summary.archival_abstract: This original reporting published by Hologram News Network (HNN) on 2022-05-10 documents “La Sogmiana | The Hologram.” Its surviving text addresses economy, lore. The record is preserved as contemporary evidence of how HNN presented the subject at publication time; factual and forward-looking claims remain attributed to the publication and require reconciliation with primary or later sources.
summary.historical_value: Preserves the HNN publication as contemporary community journalism and evidence of the network's editorial record.
summary.current_validity_warning: Claims reflect the publication date and may have been superseded, corrected, or disproven.

article_text:
La Sogmiana
Posted by Ivan | May 10, 2022 | International, Spanish
La luz se filtró por la ventana, era naranja y danzante, la niña despertó ajetreada por las detonaciones que se percibían cada vez más cerca, tímida, se escurrió hasta la ventana, entrecerrando los ojos por el brillo y las ráfagas tibias que azotaron su rostro cuando se asomó. Al enfocar su mirada, quedo inmovilizada unos segundos mientras atestiguaba como las lenguas llameantes engullían el poblado.
De un portazo entro una mujer sogmiana en su habitación,
Debemos irnos – mascullo con lágrimas en los ojos.
¿Y papa…? – reclamó la niña.
Por un segundo la mujer cerro los ojos dolorosamente. Y repitió:
No hay tiempo.
La niña había oído a sus padres hablar sobre los Adeptos de Ahr, pero en el poblado no veían plausible que corrieran peligro, solo eran rumores de viajeros, la secta que usaba como excusa la religión para comportarse como piratas contra cualquiera que no fuera creyente, era improbable que llegaran a un poblado aislado. Ahora pagaban las consecuencias por ignorar los rumores.
La mujer y la niña abandonaron la casa apenas con sus ropas, una mochila que guardaba una botella de agua y algunos alimentos junto a un cuchillo, apresuradas renunciaron a el resto de sus pertenecías a excepción de un libro de mitología griega que alcanzo a tomar la niña antes de abandonar su cuarto.
Ensordecedoras detonaciones se mezclaban con quejidos y alaridos que se percibían de los alrededores, la mujer sabía que para huir tenía más probabilidades si llegaba al bosque que bordeada el poblado, hacia el oeste.
Mientras se acercaban al borde, les alcanzo un grito, “allá hay dos”, acompañado de una ráfaga de detonaciones hacia su dirección, la madre tomo la mano de la niña jalándola, obligándola a correr al límite que le permitía su cuerpo, para poco después presenciar como la madre, seguido de un sordo quejido, caía sobre el pasto.
La niña, entumecida miraba a la mujer mientras esta, con el brazo tembloroso le alcanzaba la mochila, por unos segundos la niña olvido la persecución y las descargas, naufragó en un limbo mientras su madre la miraba paralizada, la madre corto la enajenación en la que se había sumido su hija gritándole “CORRE” con su ultimo arresto.
La niña tomo la mochila, y huyo, mientras se adentraba en el bosque miro con el rabillo del ojo como los perpetradores llegaron al cuerpo de su madre, y escucho a alguien vociferar “Ve por la niña, los demás conmigo”
Amaba leer, sobre todo historia homéricas, fantasías donde hombres se enfrentaban a amenazas increíbles e insuperables, la mitología griega la engatusaba, porque a pesar de desarrollarse en un mundo fantástico con dioses inmortales y criaturas recónditas, los héroes podrían vencer o perecer a partes iguales. Fascinada, estudió el ascenso y caída de héroes como Aquiles y Hércules, pero su favorito era Ulises, que con su agudeza supero innumerables obstáculos.
Pero ahora no la ayudarían héroes, con todo su corazón deseaba que la rescatara un héroe griego, pero esto no ocurriría, abrumada solo podía pensar en adentrarse en la espesura del bosque mientras levantaba con sus pasos las hojas secas que cubrían la tierra delatándole a su perseguidor el camino, quien aventajado con una linterna en su arma, le seguía.
Las piernas comenzaron a fallarle a la niña, tropezando con una rama suelta. Al caer sobre sus rodillas, entendía que no lograría escapar de su perseguidor. Intento idear una forma de ocultarse, pero nada se le ocurrió, aunque anteriormente había logrado sacar distancia de su perseguidor, podía ver como la luz de la linterna ahora se le acercaba hasta inevitablemente encontrarla.
Segundos después el perseguidor, un hombre delgado, ajetreado y aferrado a su rifle, encontró a la niña sentada, con su espalda recostada a un enorme árbol y sobre una alfombra de hojas secas, sollozando por lo inevitable. El hombre se acerco a la sogmiana, colgando a su espalda el rifle y sacando de su bota un cuchillo militar. Respiró profundamente antes de posicionarse frente a la niña. Esta, abrazaba la mochila, con lágrimas en los ojos levanto la mirada para cruzarla con la del hombre.
No es personal pequeña será rápido -dijo con su ronca voz.- se arrodillo frente a la niña empuñando el cuchillo. – dime algo antes, ¿haz visto un Photoli?
Aterrada la niña sogmiana apenas alcanzo a negar ligeramente con la cabeza. Al ver la reacción, el hombre miro a un lado haciendo un ligero gesto de decepción. Se miraron nuevamente mi mientras ambos escucharon la cuchilla penetrar la carne. Los ojos de la sogmiana derramaron lagrimas mientras el hombre, sin inmutarse miro hacia abajo verificando con sus ojos lo que sintió. Soltó el cuchillo militar, mientras intentó parar la hemorragia en su pecho. Mientras veía al hombre desvanecerse sobre la alfombra de hojas secas, nerviosa, la niña guardo el cuchillo sangrentado en su bolso y tomo la linterna sobre el rifle, también una pistola que colgaba el hombre en su pierna y una granada de energía antes de andar al oeste.
Por horas el silencio del bosque incito a la niña a enfrascarse en los acontecimientos que habían ocurrido hacia poco, era imposible quitar de su mente la imagen de su madre yaciendo en el pasto. Mientras se abría paso en la espesura del bosque, pudo ver un reflejo de luz amarilla latiendo a metros. Con cautela, la sogmiana se acercó, cuando estuvo a pocos pasos, pudo ver un cuerpo carnoso con extremidades delgadas que emitía una radiante luz entre sus bultos, durezas y extremidades, la luz parpadeaba con cada respiración. La niña se acercó sin entender lo que veía pero al ver al ser indefenso, no sintió miedo ni nervios, solo rodeó al ser sin reconocer donde tiene boca u ojos el ser.
El ser alzo su delgado brazo derecho, mostrando la palma de su extremidad, la sogmiana imito el gesto con su mano izquierda, extendiendo sus escuálidos dedos, acerándola al del ser. Cuando ambas palmas se rozaron, la niña absorbió una cascada de imágenes que saltaron ávidamente de una a otra, un haz de luz, jeroglíficos tallados en roca, una flota de naves, un planeta estallando, un símbolo blanco en un uniforme, un ser radiante flotando, un cielo iluminado proyectiles de energía. Cuando las imágenes de detuvieron la niña se vio arrodillada respirando agitada. Con su otra extremidad, el ser apunto al oeste disparando de su dedo un intenso haz de luz que corto la oscuridad del bosque por un instante y se perdió en la distancia, de inmediato el ser dejó de emitir la luz intermitente desde dentro de su cuerpo y paro de moverse, la niña no entendía lo que acababa de ocurrir, pero entendió que cualquiera en la extensión del bosque pudo ver el haz de luz. Apresurada la niña reanudo la marcha. La irradiación del sol naciente empezó a filtrarse entre las copas de los árboles, permitiendo a la sogmiana dilucidar el páramo tras el bosque.
Cuando alcanzo el paramo pudo escuchar motores sobrevolando detrás de ella, y corrió esperando lo peor, cuando miro hacia atrás y pudo ver a lo lejos cuatro naves cambia formas configuradas en modo de ataque sobrevolando el bosque y que se acercaban al paramo.
Corrió con todas sus fuerzas sin dilucidar donde refugiarse u ocultarse. Desde el cielo un zumbido masivo surgió, repentino, la niña miro al cielo al momento que dilucido como las nubes fueron cercenadas por una enorme nave de colores verdes y azulados que le recordaron los sembradíos del extinto poblado, la niña quedo petrificada viendo como la fortaleza flotante ensombreció el páramo disparando con precisión proyectiles que instantáneamente destruyeron las naves cambia formas. La niña perdió la fuerza de sus piernas cayendo al suelo sobre sus rodillas y codos, confundida.
Una plataforma descendió desde la nave transportando un grupo de soldados a tierra. Rápidamente frente a la niña se irguieron dos soldados apuntándole con rifles. Agotada, la niña tanteo dentro de la mochila la granada de energía.
Bajen las armas – Se escucho de detrás de los soldados mientras estos dejaron de apuntar a la niña.
Era otro hombre con el mismo uniforme pero sin casco, quien se acercó a la niña.
Tranquila, no somos piratas – Dijo.
La sogmiana observo que era un hombre alto, con cabello y barba rojiza y ojos claros. El hombre abrió las palmas de sus manos para demostrar que estaba desarmado.
Déjame ayudarte – Soltó el hombre mientras le tendía la mano.
La sogmiana abandono la granada dentro de su mochila y tomó la mano del hombre para ponerse de pie. Camino junto al hombre hacia la plataforma, a su alrededor los soldados se desplegaron en el páramo, vio el rostro del hombre que esta vez solo le sonrió, y luego notó que en su uniforme resaltaba un símbolo blanco que le recordó una de las imágenes en su cabeza. El hombre notó que la niña miraba fijamente el símbolo blanco.
Es una Quimera. – aclaro. – Bienvenida. – Agrego antes de que la plataforma los ascendiera a la nave.
The Sogmian Girl
The light filtered through the window, it was orange and dancing. The girl woke up, shaken by the detonations that were perceived closer and closer. Timidly, she slipped to the window, squinting at the brightness and the warm blasts that hit her face when she looked out. Focusing her gaze, she froze for a few seconds as she witnessed the flaming tongues engulf the village.With a slam of the door, a Sogmian woman entered her room,
We must go – she mumbled with tears in her eyes.And father…? – demanded the girl.
For a second the woman closed her eyes painfully. And repeated:
There is no time.
The girl had heard her parents talk about the Adepts of Ahr, but in the village they did not see any plausibility that they were in danger. There were only rumors of travelers, the sect that used religion as an excuse to behave like pirates against anyone who was not a believer and it was unlikely that they would come to an isolated village. Now they were paying the consequences for ignoring those rumors.
The woman and the girl left the house with only their clothes, a backpack that held a bottle of water and some food along with a knife. Hurriedly, they gave up the rest of their belongings except for a book of Greek mythology that the girl managed to take before leaving her room.Deafening detonations were mixed with moans and screams that could be heard from the surroundings, the woman knew that she had a better chance to flee if she reached the forest that bordered the village towards the west.
As they approached the edge, a shout reached them, “There are two”, accompanied by a burst of gunshots in their direction. The mother grabbed the girl’s hand pulling her, forcing her to run as far as her body would allow her to run. Shortly after, the girl witnessed how the mother, with a muffled whimper, fell on the grass.
The girl, numb, looked at the woman while she, with a trembling arm, reached for her backpack. For a few seconds the girl forgot the chase and the shock. She was shipwrecked in a limbo while her mother looked at her paralyzed and still. Her mother cut the stupefication in which her daughter had plunged by shouting “RUN” with her last breath.
The girl took the backpack, and fled. As she went into the woods she watched out of the corner of her eye as the perpetrators reached her mother’s body, and heard someone shout “Go get the girl, the rest of you with me”.
She loved to read, especially Homeric stories, fantasies where men faced incredible and insurmountable threats, Greek mythology enchanted her, because despite taking place in a fantastic world with immortal gods and hidden creatures, the heroes could win or perish in equal parts. Fascinated, she studied the rise and fall of heroes such as Achilles and Hercules, but her favorite was Odysseus, who overcame innumerable obstacles with his acumen.
But now she would not be helped by heroes. With all her heart she wished to be rescued by a Greek hero, but this would not happen. Overwhelmed she could only think of going into the thick forest. Her steps lifted the dry leaves that covered the ground giving away her path to her pursuer, who followed her with a flashlight in his weapon.
After a while, the girl’s legs began to fail her and she stumbled over a loose branch. Falling to her knees, she understood that she would not escape from her pursuer. She tried to devise a way to hide, but nothing came to mind. Although she had previously managed to get some distance from her pursuer, she could see how the light of the flashlight was now approaching her until it inevitably found her.
Seconds later the pursuer, a thin, hectic man clutching his rifle, found the girl sitting, her back leaning against a huge tree and on a carpet of dry leaves, sobbing at the inevitable to come. The man approached the Sogmian, slinging the rifle on his back and pulled a military knife from his boot. He took a deep breath before positioning himself in front of the girl. The Sogmian girl, hugging the backpack with tears in her eyes, raised her gaze to cross it with the man.
“It’s not personal, little one, it will be quick,” he said in his hoarse voice as he knelt down in front of the girl, wielding the knife.“Tell me something first, have you seen a Photoli?”
Terrified, the Sogmian girl barely managed to shake her head slightly. Seeing the reaction, the man looked to the side with a slight gesture of disappointment. They looked at each other again as they both heard the blade penetrate flesh. The Sogmian’s eyes shed tears as the man, undeterred, looked down and verifying with his eyes what he felt. He let go of the military knife, as he tried to stop the bleeding in his chest. As she watched the man fade away on the carpet of dry leaves, nervously, the girl put the bleeding knife in her bag and took the flashlight over the rifle. She also took a pistol that hung on the man’s leg and an energy grenade before walking west.
For hours, the silence of the forest forced the girl to become engrossed in the events that had just occurred. It was impossible to get the image of her mother lying in the grass out of her mind. As she made her way through the thick forest, she caught a glimpse of yellow light pulsing meters away. Cautiously, the Sogmian approached, when she was a few steps away, she could see a fleshy body with slender limbs emitting a radiant light between her form, shell, and limbs, the light flickering with each breath. The girl approached without understanding what she saw, but when she saw the defenseless being, she did not feel fear or nervous. She approached the being without being able to recognizing if the being had a mouth or eyes.
The being raised its thin right arm, showing the palm of its extremity. The Sogmian imitated the gesture with her left hand, extending her scrawny fingers, bringing it closer to the being. When both palms brushed, the girl absorbed a cascade of images that eagerly jumped from one to the other. A beam of light, hieroglyphs carved in rock, a fleet of ships, a planet exploding, a white symbol on a uniform, a radiant being floating, a sky illuminated by energy projectiles. When the images stopped, the girl saw herself kneeling, breathing heavily. With her other limb, the being pointed to the west shooting from her finger an intense beam of light that cut the darkness of the forest. For an instant, she was lost in the distance. Immediately the being stopped emitting the light from her body and stopped moving. The girl did not understand what had just happened, but she understood that anyone in the forest could have see the beam of light. Hurriedly the girl resumed her march. The irradiation of the rising sun began to filter through the treetops, allowing the Sogmian to see the moor behind the forest.
When she reached the moor she could suddenly hear engines flying behind her. She began to run expecting the worst. When she looked back and could see in the distance four shape-shifting ships configured in attack mode flying over the forest and approaching the moor.
She ran with all her might, not knowing where to take shelter or hide. From the sky a massive buzzing sound emerged. Suddenly, the girl looked at the sky as the clouds above her were cut by a huge ship of green and blue colors that reminded her of the fields of her now extinct village. The girl was petrified, watching as the newly appeared floating fortress overshadowed the wasteland, shooting precision projectiles that instantly destroyed the approaching shape-shifting ships. The girl lost the strength in her legs and fell to the ground on her knees and elbows in confusion.
A platform descended from the colorful ship carrying a group of soldiers to the ground. Quickly in front of the girl stood two soldiers pointing rifles at her. Exhausted, the girl groped inside her backpack for the energy grenade.
“Lower your weapons” was heard from behind the soldiers as they stopped aiming at the girl.
It was another man with the same uniform as the soldiers but without a helmet who approached the girl.
“Please calm down, we are not pirates”, he said.
The Sogmian observed that he was a tall man, with reddish hair and beard and light eyes. The man opened his palms to show that he was unarmed.
“Let me help you” the man said as he held out his hand.
The Sogmian abandoned the grenade in her backpack and took the man’s hand to stand up. She walked with the man to the platform. Around her the soldiers spread out on the moor. She saw the face of the man who this time only smiled at her. She then noticed that on his uniform stood out a white symbol that reminded her of one of the images in her head. The man noticed that the girl was staring at the white symbol.
“It’s a Chimera” he clarified.“Welcome” he added before the platform ascended them to the ship.
